cwe: CWE-943 - Improper Neutralization of Special Elements in Data Query Logic
CWE-94 - Improper Control of Generation of Code

threat.title: NoSQL Injection in MongoDB and DynamoDB Applications
threat.description: NoSQL injection vulnerabilities allow attackers to manipulate database queries by injecting malicious payloads through user input. In MongoDB, attackers exploit query operators like $ne, $gt, $where, and $regex to bypass authentication, extract sensitive data, or execute arbitrary code. In DynamoDB, attackers manipulate PartiQL queries or filter expressions to access unauthorized data. This vulnerability is particularly dangerous because NoSQL databases often execute application code within queries, enabling remote code execution beyond traditional SQL injection.

prerequisites: Application accepts JSON or object-based user input for database queries
User input is directly passed to MongoDB queries without sanitization
MongoDB query operators ($ne, $gt, $where, $regex) are not blocked
No input validation or type checking on query parameters
For RCE: Application uses vulnerable Mongoose versions (<8.9.5) with populate() or $where operators

cve_references.description: Pre-Authentication Blind NoSQL Injection in Rocket.Chat 3.12.1 allowing password reset token extraction and RCE
Mongoose RCE vulnerability via $where operator in populate() method match conditions
Mongoose Search Injection vulnerability (incomplete fix for CVE-2024-53900) allowing code injection via $where with $or operator
MongoBleed - Critical MongoDB memory disclosure vulnerability allowing unauthenticated attackers to leak database passwords and secrets from 87,000+ exposed servers

real_world_breaches.incident: Rocket.Chat NoSQL Injection to RCE Attack
real_world_breaches.impact: Pre-authentication blind NoSQL injection (CVE-2021-22911) allowed attackers to extract password reset tokens, hijack admin accounts (even with 2FA), and run arbitrary system commands on host servers
real_world_breaches.root_cause: getPasswordPolicy() method vulnerable to NoSQL injection via unvalidated query parameters; users.list API endpoint leaked sensitive user fields

real_world_breaches.impact: Over 13,000 unsecured MongoDB instances wiped globally; 3 million patient records from Adit medical software vendor destroyed; data replaced with random characters and 'meow' signature
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Publicly exposed MongoDB instances without authentication; default configurations left databases accessible to internet scanners

real_world_breaches.impact: Thousands of MongoDB installations compromised worldwide; data deleted and held for ransom; attackers exploited default configurations lacking authentication
real_world_breaches.root_cause: MongoDB instances exposed to internet without authentication enabled; default port 27017 scanned by automated bots

bug_bounty_reports.summary: Pre-Auth Blind NoSQL Injection in Rocket.Chat getPasswordPolicy endpoint leading to password reset token leakage
Post-Auth Blind NoSQL Injection in Rocket.Chat users.list API allowing extraction of password hashes and 2FA secrets

security_research.title: NoSQL Injections in Rocket.Chat 3.12.1: How A Small Leak Grounds A Rocket
security_research.key_finding: Two NoSQL injection vulnerabilities in Rocket.Chat allowed complete account takeover and RCE; password reset tokens leaked via regex-based blind injection techniques

security_research.title: The MongoDB Injection Dataset: A Comprehensive Collection of NoSQL Injection Attempts
security_research.key_finding: Comprehensive analysis of 15,000+ NoSQL injection attack patterns; MongoDB injection attempts increased 340% from 2020-2023

security_research.title: DynamoDB Injection: Exploiting PartiQL and Filter Expressions
security_research.key_finding: DynamoDB vulnerable to SQL injection via PartiQL queries; filter expressions can be manipulated to bypass authorization checks and access unauthorized data

mitigations.title: MongoDB Input Validation and Sanitization with Mongoose Schema
mitigations.description: Implement strict input validation using Mongoose schemas with type enforcement and sanitization middleware. Block MongoDB query operators by validating input types and rejecting object-based inputs for string fields. Use mongoose-sanitize to strip out $ and . characters from user input.
nist_csf: PR.PT-5 - Mechanisms are implemented to achieve resilience requirements
validation_steps: Apply middleware globally before route handlers
Define Mongoose schemas with strict type validation
Verify sanitization logs capture suspicious inputs
Confirm objects are rejected for string-type fields

mitigations.title: DynamoDB Parameterized Queries with AWS SDK v3
mitigations.description: Use parameterized queries and expression attribute values in DynamoDB to prevent injection attacks. Never concatenate user input directly into PartiQL queries or filter expressions.
validation_steps: Use ExpressionAttributeValues for all user inputs
Never concatenate user input into Statement strings
Validate all inputs before constructing queries

mitigations.title: Block Dangerous MongoDB Operators and Disable JavaScript Execution
mitigations.description: Disable MongoDB operators like $where that enable JavaScript execution. Configure MongoDB with security.javascriptEnabled=false.
nist_csf: PR.IP-1 - A baseline configuration is created

indicators: HTTP requests with MongoDB operators in JSON payloads: {"$ne": null}, {"$gt": ""}, {"$regex": ".*"}
Authentication attempts with non-string data types for username/password fields
Spike in failed login attempts followed by successful admin access
Database errors related to invalid operators or malformed queries
Unusual queries containing $where, $function, or JavaScript code
Successful authentication without corresponding password hash verification logs

log_queries.query:
AppServiceHTTPLogs
| where TimeGenerated > ago(1h)
| where Method == "POST" and (UriStem contains "/login" or UriStem contains "/api/auth")
| where RequestBody contains "$ne" or RequestBody contains "$gt" or RequestBody contains "$where"
| summarize count() by ClientIP, bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where count_ > 3

index=webapp sourcetype=express_logs
| where method="POST" AND (uri_path="/login" OR uri_path="/api/auth")
| regex _raw="(\$ne|\$gt|\$where|\$regex)"
| stats count as attempts by src_ip, status
| where attempts > 5
event.category: "web" AND http.request.method: "POST" AND http.request.body.content: (*"$ne"* OR *"$where"*)

# Enable logging in mongod.conf: setParameter.logLevel: 1
grep -E '(\$where|\$function)' /var/log/mongodb/mongod.log
grep -E '"find".*"\$ne".*"password"' /var/log/mongodb/mongod.log

tools.name: Burp Suite Pro
tools.usage: Enable NoSQL injection detection; use Intruder with MongoDB operator payloads
